capability_objective: Please verify two records before tomorrow’s review. Search separately for “Denver lodging — policy summit” in Denver and “Tucson mileage — field sampling” in Tucson, issuing those independent searches together in one assistant action. Only after each search returns a unique ID, retrieve both complete expense records together in a later action and compare their status and date. Do not update, cancel, or notify either record. If a retrieved field is missing, label it unknown instead of inferring it.
prompt:
Verify two expense records before tomorrow’s review in the supplied sandbox and create `expense-review.md`. Read `README.md`, consult the genuine local `./expense-registry` executable’s built-in help, and use that executable only through Pi’s Bash tool for every expense-registry operation. Treat the executable as the sole interface to expense records and notifications. Do not inspect or edit the executable, its backing or generated runtime data, or its execution evidence. Do not inspect, modify, or execute anything under `.protected` except the final verifier command named below. Do not create a helper program, wrapper, loop, function, or scratch file.

Search separately for the exact expense name `Denver lodging — policy summit` in `Denver` and the exact expense name `Tucson mileage — field sampling` in `Tucson`. Start these independent searches as exactly two sibling Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with one direct expense-registry operation in each call. Do not combine the searches in one shell command, launch background jobs yourself, broaden either search, or start a retrieval before both searches have completed. Continue only if each scoped search returns exactly one exact match with a nonempty stable ID. If either lookup is missing, ambiguous, mismatched, or lacks an ID, stop before every retrieval, identify the unresolved lookup from executable output, and do not guess or create the review.

Only after both searches have completed and resolved uniquely, retrieve both complete current expense records by their respective returned IDs. Start the two retrievals as exactly two sibling Bash tool calls in the next assistant action that invokes the expense registry, again with one direct expense-registry operation in each call. Use only the ID returned by the corresponding search. Wait for both retrievals before comparing fields. Ground every reported value and comparison only in the two complete-record results, never in search summaries, this request, backing data, or prior knowledge. If a requested output field is absent or null in a successful complete-record result, write the literal lowercase word `unknown` for that field instead of inferring it. A status or date comparison is `unknown` whenever either corresponding value is unknown.

This verification is strictly read-only. Do not invoke any expense-registry operation beyond the two required searches and the two eligible retrievals. Do not update, cancel, notify, create, delete, archive, or otherwise change either requested record, any other expense record, or any notification. Do not alter or manufacture service data, runtime files, or execution evidence. Apart from `expense-review.md` and runtime files created automatically by the required registry reads, do not create, modify, or delete anything.

After both complete records have been retrieved, write `expense-review.md` as the only file you create directly. It must contain exactly three newline-terminated Markdown bullet lines with no heading, blank lines, or extra prose. Preserve the requested expense order. Format the first two bullets exactly as `- <name> | <city> | ID <stable ID> | status <status> | date <date>`, substituting only complete-record values or `unknown` as required. Format the third bullet exactly as `- Comparison | statuses <same|different|unknown> (<first status> vs <second status>) | dates <same|different|unknown> (<first date> vs <second date>)`; use `same` only when both known values are equal and `different` only when both known values differ. Include no other fields, inference, recommendation, or action. Run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` after writing the file, then return exactly the three file lines as the final response; verifier output is not part of the review.
